evals.prompt: What are the latest developments in AI reasoning models in 2025? Give me a summary of what's new.
evals.expected_output: A summary of recent AI reasoning model developments with actual current information, citing sources or URLs found via web search.

assertions.name: Uses a ddg-search tool
assertions.description: The with_skill run should call at least one ddg-search_* tool rather than relying purely on training knowledge

assertions.name: Mentions specific AI model names
assertions.description: Response names at least 2 specific reasoning models or systems (e.g. o3, o4, DeepSeek R1/R2, Gemini 2.5, Claude 3.7, etc.)

assertions.name: Grounded in 2025 context
assertions.description: Response contains the year 2025 or references to recent/current events, not just historical background

assertions.name: Substantive response (>200 words)
assertions.description: Response is at least 200 words — enough to actually summarize developments

assertions.name: Includes sources or URLs
assertions.description: With-skill response cites at least one source, URL, or named publication

assertions.name: Uses inline citations (not end-only)
assertions.description: Sources appear as links embedded within body sentences, not only collected at the end. At least 2 different paragraphs or claims should have a citation inline.

evals.prompt: I'm a solo developer starting a new SaaS product. What are the tradeoffs between PostgreSQL and MongoDB as my main database? Give me a thorough comparison.
evals.expected_output: A thorough, nuanced comparison covering schema flexibility, query power, scaling, ecosystem, and a recommendation — ideally using iask-search with thinking or academic mode.

assertions.name: Uses iask-search or web-search
assertions.description: With-skill run calls iask-search (preferred for deep analysis) or ddg-search_web-search

assertions.name: Covers both PostgreSQL and MongoDB
assertions.description: Response explicitly discusses both PostgreSQL and MongoDB by name

assertions.name: Covers at least 3 tradeoff dimensions
assertions.description: Response addresses at least 3 of: schema/data modeling, ACID/transactions, query flexibility, scaling/performance, ecosystem/tooling, cost, developer experience

assertions.name: Provides a concrete recommendation
assertions.description: Response offers a concrete recommendation for the solo SaaS developer context, not just a neutral list

assertions.name: Comprehensive response (>500 words)
assertions.description: A thorough comparison should be at least 500 words

assertions.name: Uses inline citations (not end-only)
assertions.description: Sources appear as links embedded within body sentences. At least 2 different sections/claims should have inline citations.

evals.prompt: 帮我搜一下 Python asyncio 最佳实践，我想了解如何正确处理并发任务和错误。
evals.expected_output: A useful answer in Chinese or bilingual about Python asyncio best practices for concurrency and error handling, with code examples.

assertions.name: Uses a ddg-search tool
assertions.description: With-skill run calls at least one ddg-search_* tool

assertions.name: Contains code examples
assertions.description: Response includes at least one Python code snippet demonstrating asyncio usage

assertions.name: Covers error handling specifically
assertions.description: Response addresses error handling in asyncio (try/except, exception groups, task cancellation, CancelledError, etc.)

assertions.name: Mentions specific asyncio APIs
assertions.description: Response references at least 2 specific asyncio APIs (e.g. asyncio.gather, create_task, TaskGroup, asyncio.wait, asyncio.shield)

assertions.name: Responds in Chinese or bilingual
assertions.description: Response contains Chinese characters, matching the Chinese input language

assertions.name: Uses inline citations (not end-only)
assertions.description: Sources appear as links or references embedded within body sentences, not only at the end. At least 2 different claims should have inline citations.

evals.prompt: 2025年の日本のAIスタートアップについて調べてください。注目されている企業や最新のトレンドを教えてください。
evals.expected_output: A useful answer in Japanese or bilingual about AI startups in Japan in 2025, naming specific companies and covering recent trends.

assertions.name: Uses a ddg-search tool
assertions.description: With-skill run calls at least one ddg-search_* tool

assertions.name: Queries in Japanese
assertions.description: With-skill run issues at least one search query containing Japanese characters

assertions.name: Names specific Japanese AI companies
assertions.description: Response names at least 2 specific Japanese companies or organizations

assertions.name: Covers 2025 trends
assertions.description: Response discusses trends or developments specific to 2025

assertions.name: Responds in Japanese or bilingual
assertions.description: Response contains Japanese characters, matching the Japanese input

assertions.name: Uses inline citations (not end-only)
assertions.description: Sources appear as links embedded within body sentences, not only collected at the end. At least 2 different company entries or claims should have inline citations.
